text.title: Tailwind Anti-patterns
text.summary: Failure modes that generate broken, unmaintainable, or purge-unsafe Tailwind code.

antipattern.description: Arbitrary values used freely: text-[#1a1a1a], w-[37px], mt-[13px].
antipattern.reason: Agents regenerate plausible hex codes from training data, bypassing the design system. Values get stripped by PurgeCSS when dynamically composed.

antipattern.description: Duplicating base styles into every variant instead of the cva base array.
antipattern.reason: LLMs copy focus-visible and disabled states into each variant, creating multi-kilobyte className strings and noisy diffs.
JIT cannot statically analyze interpolated strings; classes get purged at build time and are silently missing at runtime.

antipattern.description: Adding bg-white without dark:bg-zinc-900 (dark mode omission).
antipattern.reason: Agents add light-mode styles without dark: pairs; requires a full dark-mode audit to fix retroactively.

antipattern.description: Long className strings repeated across 3+ files without extraction.
antipattern.reason: Duplication forks design tokens silently; changing one instance leaves the others stale.

rule.statement: Run eslint-plugin-tailwindcss (classnames-order, no-custom-classname, no-contradicting-classname) and prettier-plugin-tailwindcss on every PR.
rule.rationale: Automated enforcement catches ordering drift, unknown classes, and conflicting utilities before they reach main.

text.reference: Script to find className strings duplicated across 3+ components
